'''Dinko''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] colonist who lived in [[Fort Nowhere]] on [[Ruusan/Legends|Ruusan]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. 

In [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]], he served as a the number-three gunner, manning one of the [[blaster cannon/Legends|blaster cannon]] turrets of [[Fort Nowhere]] when he and [[Marie Peeno]] saw an [[Imperial Remnant|Imperial]] [[assault shuttle/Legends|assault shuttle]] approaching, validating [[Grif Grawley]]'s warnings that the Empire had discovered the [[planet/Legends|planet]]. Dinko asked permission to shoot at it, but Peeno countermanded him, being sure that it was part of a [[Vengeance Battle Group|larger force]], and the Imperials only wanted to talk.<ref name="JK">''[[Dark Forces: Jedi Knight]]''</ref>

Soon after, the Imperials negotiated with the settlement and lied that they will leave it alone. Again manning the turret with Peeno he asked for an early leave as he had a date that night, and Peeno seemed relaxed enough allow him when an alarm blinked. Dinko insisted it was not the movements of the native [[Bouncer]]s: the Imperials had prepared an assault.<ref name="Dark Forces: Jedi Knight audio drama">[[Dark Forces: Jedi Knight (audio drama)|''Dark Forces: Jedi Knight'' audio drama]]</ref>

In the ensuing [[battle of Fort Nowhere]] Dinko managed to hit a [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE/LN starfighter]] who debris fell on [[Katarn's Hill]]. He was killed by Imperial forces.<ref name="JK" />

==Behind the scenes==
In the audio adaptation of [[Dark Forces: Jedi Knight (audio drama)|''Dark Forces: Jedi Knight'']], he was voiced by [[Stephen Yoakam]].
